activities.main_competence: 1.0 Manage information search from different sources for lifelong learning
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Use ICT tools to search for information from different sources
activities.learning_activity: (a) Familiarise with various types of search engines (e.g., Google, Google Scholar, Bing)
suggested_methods: Brainstorming : Guide the students through brainstorming different types of search engines and the information they can search through them
Practical: Guide the students in groups through completing web search tasks to find information on a given topic
activities.assessment_criteria: Various types of search engines are familiarised effectively

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Manage information search from different sources for lifelong learning
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Use ICT tools to search for information from different sources
activities.learning_activity: (b) Utilise ICT tools to search for general information (e.g., books, journals)
suggested_methods: Practical: Guide the students through completing web search tasks to find books, journals, articles, and images and storing them for use
activities.assessment_criteria: ICT tools are utilised effectively in searching for general information

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 2.1. Develop listening skills
activities.learning_activity: (a) Answer questions based on information from presentations/tasks
suggested_methods: Presentation: Make a presentation on a selected topic to the students and ask them questions
Guide the students in groups through presenting topics and responding to questions from others’ presentations
activities.assessment_criteria: Questions based on information from presentations/tasks are answered correctly
suggested_resources: Texts about a wide range of events

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 2.1. Develop listening skills
activities.learning_activity: (b) Practise pronunciation of words from oral and audio/audio-visual sources
suggested_methods: Tongue twisters: Play recorded tongue twisters for students to listen and note down new words they learned to pronounce
Reciting: Guide the students through reciting the tongue twisters by pronouncing words correctly
Songs: Play recorded authentic songs with the appropriate pronunciations for students to listen. Then, guide the students through singing the songs by pronouncing words correctly
activities.assessment_criteria: Pronunciation of words from oral and audio/audio-visual sources is practised appropriately

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 2.1. Develop listening skills
activities.learning_activity: (c) Write words heard from oral and audio/audio-visual sources
suggested_methods: Dictation: Guide the students through writing the words heard from audio/audio-visual recordings
Pair work: Guide the students in pairs through writing sentences using the words heard
activities.assessment_criteria: Words heard from oral and audio/audio-visual sources are written

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 2.1. Develop listening skills
activities.learning_activity: (d) Reproduce messages from oral presentations
suggested_methods: Imitation: Guide the students through reproducing messages from the information presented orally
Whispering game/Message passing : Guide the students through listening to oral information and passing it on to others
activities.assessment_criteria: Messages from oral presentations are reproduced accurately

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Produce short and coherent oral messages with intelligible pronunciation and fluency
activities.learning_activity: (a) Distinguish words with minimal sound distinctions that have different meanings (cut/cat; paper/pepper; date/debt)
suggested_methods: Word cards: Use an appropriate number of cards to write minimal pairs and ask the students to distinguish their sounds and meanings
Tongue twisters : Guide the students through pronouncing tongue twisters with minimal pairs

Minimal pairs games: Guide the students through pronouncing words with nearly similar sounds by using games (e.g., Is it a pair ?, odd one out , minimal pair up, minimal pair slap , minimal pairs pelmanism, and pronunciation journey)
activities.assessment_criteria: Words with minimal sound distinctions that have different meanings are distinguished correctly

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Produce short and coherent oral messages with intelligible pronunciation and fluency
activities.learning_activity: (b) Practise pronunciation with proper word stress assignment
suggested_methods: Mimic : Use ICT to play word pronunciation with appropriate stress placement and ask the students to mimic
Dictionary use : Use a dictionary to show the students how to indicate stress on syllables of words, and guide them through pronouncing the words
Games: Use games like Clap or Clicking fingers on the stresse d syllables and mumbling stress pattern
activities.assessment_criteria: Pronunciation with proper word stress assignment is practised appropriately

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Produce short and coherent oral messages with intelligible pronunciation and fluency
activities.learning_activity: (c) Practise pronunciation of words with short and long vowels (e.g., sit/seat, hit/heat, pot/port)
suggested_methods: Mimic: Use ICT to play pronunciation of words with short and long vowels and ask the students to mimic
Games: Use games like ‘Same or Different’ and ‘Pronunciation Maze’ to guide the students through pronouncing words with short and long vowels
activities.assessment_criteria: Pronunciation of words with short and long vowels is practised appropriately

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Produce short and coherent oral messages with intelligible pronunciation and fluency
activities.learning_activity: (d) Practise pronunciation of unfamiliar sounds (e.g., through the use of tongue twisters, songs, poems)
suggested_methods: Information search: Guide the students through searching for unfamiliar words from different sources and presenting their work to the class
Tongue twisters: Guide the students through pronouncing tongue twisters with unfamiliar sounds
Songs: Guide the students through singing a song with unfamiliar sounds
Poems: Guide the students through reading aloud and reciting poems with unfamiliar sounds
Mimic: Use ICT to play the pronunciation of unfamiliar sounds and ask the students to mimic
activities.assessment_criteria: Pronunciation of unfamiliar sounds is practised appropriately through the use of various techniques

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Produce short and coherent oral messages with intelligible pronunciation and fluency
activities.learning_activity: (e) Practise pronunciation of words with similar sounds that have different spellings (e.g., one/won, ewe/you)
suggested_methods: Drill: Guide the students through choral and individual repetition to pronounce correctly words with similar sounds
Games: Guide the students through pronouncing words with similar sounds but different spellings by using games (e.g., Is it a pair?, odd one out, pelmanism and homophone go fish)
activities.assessment_criteria: Pronunciation of words with similar sounds but different spellings is practised appropriately

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 2.3 Develop vocabulary from conversations and written texts
activities.learning_activity: (a) Write new vocabulary from conversations and written texts on particular topics (e.g., politics, sports, tourism, agriculture)
suggested_methods: Discussion: Guide the students through discussing a particular topic and writing the vocabulary heard and their meanings
Dialogue: Guide the students through participating in a dialogue and writing new vocabulary from it
Extensive reading: Guide the students through reading texts to acquire vocabulary for specific fields and writing new vocabulary
activities.assessment_criteria: New vocabulary from conversations and written texts on particular topics are written correctly

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 2.3 Develop vocabulary from conversations and written texts
activities.learning_activity: (b) Construct sentences using vocabulary acquired from conversations and written texts
suggested_methods: Dialogue: Guide the students through acting out a dialogue and constructing sentences using the new vocabulary learned
Debate: Guide the students through participating in a debate and constructing sentences using the new vocabulary learned
Spotting words : Guide the students through identifying new vocabulary from written texts and constructing sentences using the words
Close reading: Guide the students through reading texts and constructing sentences using the new words learned
activities.assessment_criteria: Sentences are constructed correctly using vocabulary acquired from conversations and written texts

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 2.3 Develop vocabulary from conversations and written texts
activities.learning_activity: (c) Play vocabulary building games using digital and non-digital resources (e.g., word board games, charades, picture games, flash cards)
suggested_methods: Games: Guide the students through playing vocabulary building games
Questions and answers: Ask the students some questions about the new vocabulary they have learned
Collaborative practice : Guide the students in pairs/groups through constructing sentences using the new vocabulary they have learned
activities.assessment_criteria: V ocabulary building games are played accurately using digital and non-digital resources

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 2.4 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks
activities.learning_activity: (a) Express ideas or opinions in contextualised oral and written language tasks using proper grammar and vocabulary (e.g hospital, market, library, bus stop, sport and games)
suggested_methods: Conversations : Guide the students through expressing their opinions orally and in writing by participating in conversations (e.g., discussion, debate, dialogue, bargaining) on controversial issues
Scenario: Present some controversial cases and ask the students to discuss and express their opinions orally and in writing.
Roleplay: Guide the students through roleplaying radio/TV programmes by giving opinions on given topics
activities.assessment_criteria: Ideas or opinions are expressed correctly in contextualised oral and written language tasks using proper grammar and vocabulary
suggested_resources: Audio and audio-visual materials (audio clips, recorded stories)

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 2.4 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks
activities.learning_activity: (b) Construct sentences using appropriate grammar and vocabulary relating to a specific context (structure: tenses – simple past, simple present, articles, present progressive, pronouns, coordinators, prepositions, adjectives, adverbs)
suggested_methods: Description: Guide the students through describing objects, events, and places using appropriate grammar and vocabulary
Pair work: Guide the students in pairs through writing sentences in different tenses and discussing why the sentences are written in those tenses
Group work : Guide the students in groups through constructing sentences orally and in writing which describe qualities using pronouns, coordinators, prepositions, adjectives and adverbs
activities.assessment_criteria: Sentences are constructed correctly using appropriate grammar and vocabulary relating to a specific context

activities.main_competence: 2.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 2.4 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks
activities.learning_activity: (c) Debate on topics using proper grammar and vocabulary
suggested_methods: Debates: Guide the students through conducting debates on cross-cutting topics using proper grammar and vocabulary
activities.assessment_criteria: Topics are debated appropriately using proper grammar and vocabulary

activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Read texts for comprehension
activities.learning_activity: (a) Sequence the order of events in stories
suggested_methods: Reading stories : Guide the students through reading stories with sequence words. (e.g., next, then, after, earlier, first, second, and later) Then, ask them to identify the words and use them to write a paragraph
Filling the missing parts: Give the students stories with skipped parts and ask them to fill in the missing parts using sequence words
Strip story: Guide the students through sequencing the order of events in stories using strip story. Then, let them read their stories aloud in the classroom
Story completion: Provide the students with a beginning part of a story and guide them through completing it using sequence words
Jumbled texts : Use jumbled texts to guide the students through ordering the sequence of events in the stories
activities.assessment_criteria: The order of events in a story is sequenced logically

activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Read texts for comprehension
activities.learning_activity: (b) Make a summary of stories
suggested_methods: Discussion: Guide the students through discussing the main issues in a story
5Ws and 1H, First Then Finally, SWBST, and SAAC Method: Guide the students through summarising various stories using the aforementioned methods
activities.assessment_criteria: Stories are summarised correctly

activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Read texts for comprehension
activities.learning_activity: (c) Retell stories
suggested_methods: Retelling stories: Guide the students in pairs through retelling stories by taking turns
Narration: Guide the students through narrating to others about story books and appropriate movies they have watched
activities.assessment_criteria: Stories are retold accordingly

activities.specific_competence: 3.2 Comprehend oral messages with increasing difficulty
activities.learning_activity: (a) Paraphrase various oral messages from different contexts
suggested_methods: Paraphrasing: Read passages orally, play recorded texts and guide the students through paraphrasing the messages
Project: Ask the students to listen to various oral messages (presentations, on radio, TV) and paraphrase the messages for presentation in the classroom
activities.assessment_criteria: Various oral messages are paraphrased appropriately from different contexts
suggested_resources: audio and audio-visual materials (audio clips, recorded stories)

activities.specific_competence: 3.2 Comprehend oral messages with increasing difficulty
activities.learning_activity: (b) Respond to oral messages from different contexts
suggested_methods: Roleplay: Guide the students through roleplay to respond to oral instructions, information and orders
Telephone conversation : Guide the students through responding to interrogations via telephone conversations
activities.assessment_criteria: Oral messages from different contexts are responded to correctly

activities.specific_competence: 3.2 Comprehend oral messages with increasing difficulty
activities.learning_activity: (c) Respond to questions about oral messages
suggested_methods: Panel interviews: Guide the students through responding to questions in panel interviews
activities.assessment_criteria: Questions about oral messages are responded to correctly

activities.specific_competence: 3.3 Respond appropriately in a variety of oral and written communication contexts
activities.learning_activity: (a) Express ideas and opinions in different interpersonal communication settings (e.g., I think…, I guess…, In my opinion…, I would say …)
suggested_methods: Information search : Guide the students through seeking information on expressing ideas and opinions in interpersonal communication
Scenario: Use a controversial scenario to guide the students through expressing their opinions orally and in writing
activities.assessment_criteria: Ideas and opinions are expressed correctly in different interpersonal communication settings

activities.specific_competence: 3.3 Respond appropriately in a variety of oral and written communication contexts
activities.learning_activity: (b) Use appropriate non-verbal cues in interpersonal communication
suggested_methods: Discussion: Guide the students through discussing the meaning of different non-verbal cues and presenting the work to the class
Act it out: Guide the students in groups through acting and interpreting non-verbal cues
activities.assessment_criteria: Appropriate non-verbal cues are used correctly in interpersonal communication

activities.specific_competence: 3.3 Respond appropriately in a variety of oral and written communication contexts
activities.learning_activity: (c) Use proper grammar and vocabulary in different interpersonal communication contexts (e.g., greetings, biding farewell, apologising)
suggested_methods: Roleplay: Guide the students through roleplaying greeting guests, bidding farewell and apologising using proper grammar and vocabulary
activities.assessment_criteria: Grammar and vocabulary are used correctly in different interpersonal communication contexts

activities.specific_competence: 3.3 Respond appropriately in a variety of oral and written communication contexts
activities.learning_activity: (d) Use appropriate language and expressions in communicating with people of different age groups and statuses (e.g., peers, elders, family members, leaders)
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide the students in groups through brainstorming ideas on the differences in conversations between different groups of people
Dialogue: Guide the students through participating in dialogue among different age groups and statuses
activities.assessment_criteria: Appropriate language and expressions are used with different age groups and statuses

activities.specific_competence: 4.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary for oral communication in a variety of contexts
activities.learning_activity: (a) Express daily routines (structure: present simple)
suggested_methods: Real-life expression: Guide the students through expressing orally what they do usually
Inquiry: Guide the students through inquiring what famous people in their community do usually and presenting their work before the class
Interview: Guide the students in pairs through interviewing each other about their fact routines
activities.assessment_criteria: Daily routines are expressed correctly
suggested_resources: maps of various places (e.g., school, village)

activities.specific_competence: 4.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary for oral communication in a variety of contexts
activities.learning_activity: (b) Express ongoing activities (structure: present progressive)
suggested_methods: Description: Guide the students through describing events as they occur (e.g., wedding ceremonies, birthday parties, burial ceremonies, processes, and public events) using the present progressive tense
activities.assessment_criteria: Ongoing activities are expressed correctly

activities.specific_competence: 4.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary for oral communication in a variety of contexts
activities.learning_activity: (c) Express family relationship and occupations (structure: present simple+progressive)
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide the students in pairs through brainstorming ideas about family relationship and different kinds of occupations
Songs and games : Guide the students through singing songs and playing games about family relationships and occupations
Matching: Guide the students through matching pictures with occupations and occupations with their descriptions
The use of pictures: Display pictures of people with different occupations and ask the students to express their occupations using the present simple+progressive tenses
Real-life expression: Ask the students to express the occupations of their parents/guardians and other relatives using the present simple+progressive tenses
activities.assessment_criteria: Family relationship and occupations are expressed correctly

activities.specific_competence: 4.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary for oral communication in a variety of contexts
activities.learning_activity: (d) Express ownership/possession (structure: have/has in present simple and had in past simple, apostrophe, possessive pronouns)
suggested_methods: Information search: Guide the students through reading texts on expressing possession using have, has, had, apostrophe, and possessive pronouns
Real-life expression: Guide the students through expressing possessions using the real objects they posses
Conversation: Guide the students in groups through engaging in conversations about what their relatives and neighbours posses
activities.assessment_criteria: Ownership/possession is expressed correctly

activities.specific_competence: 4.1 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary for oral communication in a variety of contexts
activities.learning_activity: (e) Give directions
suggested_methods: Information search: Guide the students through searching for information on how to ask for and give directions and present the work to the class
Map reading: Guide the students in pairs through directing each other how to reach various stations/points (from one point to another) as indicated in the map
Group work: Guide the students in groups through asking and giving directions on how to reach various places (e.g., offices, classrooms, and toilets) within the school compound
activities.assessment_criteria: Directions are given appropriately

activities.specific_competence: 4.2 Create a variety of texts for different communicative purposes using the appropriate tone and register
activities.learning_activity: (a) Recognise the main stages of writing texts ( e.g., pre-writing, drafting, revising, editing)
suggested_methods: Discussion: Guide the students through discussing stages of writing texts and presenting their work to the class
Demonstration: Demonstrate how to begin writing by asking the students to give ideas and help you plan the text
activities.assessment_criteria: The stages of writing texts are recognised appropriately
suggested_resources: samples of friendly letters and invitations

activities.specific_competence: 4.2 Create a variety of texts for different communicative purposes using the appropriate tone and register
activities.learning_activity: (b) Write a variety of friendly letters and invitations (vocabulary: Dear Father/Uncle, Dear Mother/Aunt, Mr/Mrs…, Dr./Prof./Madam/Ms …)
suggested_methods: Observation: Display/distribute samples of friendly letters and invitations for the students to study and discover their parts
Collaboration : Guide the students through collaborating in writing friendly letters and invitations
activities.assessment_criteria: A variety of friendly letters and invitations are written correctly with appropriate vocabulary

activities.specific_competence: 4.2 Create a variety of texts for different communicative purposes using the appropriate tone and register
activities.learning_activity: (c) Create dialogues on various topics (e.g., the value of education, environment, culture, gender equality)
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide the students in groups through brainstorming ideas on the meaning and structure of dialogues
Observation: Guide the students through studying a prepared dialogue and writing down the key features they observe
Pair work: Guide the students in pairs through creating dialogues on various topics
Act it out: Guide the students in pairs through acti ng out the dialogues they have prepared
activities.assessment_criteria: Dialogues on various topics are created appropriately

activities.specific_competence: 4.2 Create a variety of texts for different communicative purposes using the appropriate tone and register
activities.learning_activity: (d) Create and express orally daily and weekly timetables for routines
suggested_methods: Real-life expression: Guide the students through expressing orally and in writing what they do weekly
Inquiry: Guide the students through inquiring what famous people do weekly and reporting to the classroom
Interview: Guide the students through interviewing each other in pairs about their fact routines and timetables
Planning and preparing : Guide the students individually through planning and preparing their w eekly timetables and presenting them to the class
activities.assessment_criteria: Daily and weekly time-tables for routines are created and expressed correctly

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Develop listening skills
activities.learning_activity: (a) Participate in various conversations in different communicative settings (e.g., peer discussions, storytelling)
suggested_methods: Discussion: Guide the students through discussing various cross-cutting issues, noting down important ideas and presenting the work to the class
Storytelling: Ask the students in pairs to tell stories by taking turns, write down important ideas and present them to the class

Dialogue: Guide the students through acting out a dialogue on various interesting events (football matches, tourism trips, visits to grandparents, and school leaders’ election) and ask them questions from the dialogue
Interview: Guide some students (inteviewers) through inteviewing others (interviewee) on various issues/topics
Round table circle : Guide the students through participating in discussion on the selected topics using the round table circle and presenting their work to the class
activities.assessment_criteria: Participations in conversations in various communicative settings is done effectively
suggested_resources: Texts of a wide range of events
pictures and recorded materials on various situations

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Develop listening skills
activities.learning_activity: (b) Summarise information from oral presentations and audio/audio-visual sources
suggested_methods: Presentation : Make a presentation on any interesting topic and ask the students to listen to it attentively
Discussion: Guide the students through discussing and summarising the information from the presentation, and presenting their work to the class

Recordings: Guide the students through summarising recorded information (e.g., recorded song, dialogue, speech and interview) played in class using digital devices (e.g., smartphone, tablet, computer, radio). Then, ask each them to present their work through gallery walk

Project activity: Ask the students to listen to and watch the news on the radio and TV respectively at home and school. Then, ask them to summarise the news broadcast and present the work to the class
activities.assessment_criteria: Information from oral presentations and audio/audio-visual sources is summarised logically

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Develop listening skills
activities.learning_activity: (c) Paraphrase information presented orally
suggested_methods: Narrating stories: Narrate an interesting story to the students, and guide them through paraphrasing information from the story and presenting their work to the class
Project activity: Guide the students through paraphrasing the news broadcast they have listened to on radio and watched on TV at home or school and presenting their work to the class
activities.assessment_criteria: Information presented orally is paraphrased correctly

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Produce short and coherent oral messages with intelligible pronunciation and fluency
activities.learning_activity: (a) Construct sentences with proper intonation
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide the students through brainstorming various communication contexts/settings (e.g., market, sports and games, hospital, restaurant/hotel) and identifying vocabulary used in such contexts/settings
Sentence construction: Guide the students through constructing sentences orally using the identified vocabulary
Scenario/case study: Guide the students through using some vocabulary and grammatical items from a scenario /case study to construct different sentences orally
activities.assessment_criteria: Sentences with proper intonation are constructed appropriately

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 1.2 Produce short and coherent oral messages with intelligible pronunciation and fluency
activities.learning_activity: (b) Use cohesive devices to deliver oral presentations of events (e.g., first of all, moreover, but, however, yet, in contrast, although, otherwise, still, on the contrary, on the other hand)
suggested_methods: Discussion: Guide the students in pairs through preparing and making presentations using cohesive devices
activities.assessment_criteria: Cohesive devices are used to deliver oral presentations logically

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 1.3 Develop vocabulary from conversations and written texts
activities.learning_activity: (a) Recognise words with opposite meanings from conversations and written texts
suggested_methods: Roleplay: Use a roleplay to guide the students in pairs or in groups through acting out different roles. (e.g., doctor and patient, teacher and student, chairperson and villagers)
TIPS: Provide the students with some words from the roleplay and guide them through TIPS to find their opposite meanings and use them to construct sentences
Hot seat: Guide the students through using the hot seat to ask and respond to questions based on the topics provided to them
Brainstorming: Ask the students to brainstorm the opposite meanings of some words used in the hot seat and use them to construct sentences
Discussion: Provide the students with texts to read, discuss their contents and make presentations
Memory card game: Guide the students through recognising words with opposite meanings from the texts read by playing the memory card game, finding their meanings and using them to construct sentences
activities.assessment_criteria: Words with opposite meanings from conversations and written texts are recognised
suggested_resources: Audio and audio-visual materials (audio/video clips, recorded stories)

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 1.3 Develop vocabulary from conversations and written texts
activities.learning_activity: (b) Use words from conversations and written texts to produce new vocabulary by attaching suffixes and prefixes (e.g., kind-unkind; slow-slowly)
suggested_methods: Talk show: Guide the students through participating in talk shows on different topics/themes and using some vocabulary from the talk shows to produce new vocabulary by attaching suffixes and prefixes and then constructing sentences using the new vocabulary learned
Prefix and suffix bingo: Use the prefix and suffix bingo to guide the students through forming new vocabulary. Then, ask them to construct sentences using the new vocabulary
activities.assessment_criteria: New vocabulary is correctly formed from conversations and written texts by attaching suffixes and prefixes

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 1.4 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks
activities.learning_activity: (a) Produce contextualised oral and written descriptions based on personal experience
suggested_methods: Description: Guide the students through describing different events, places, and living and non-living things based on their experience orally and in writing using descriptive words
activities.assessment_criteria: Oral and written descriptions based on personal experience are produced logically
suggested_resources: Audio and audio-visual materials (audio/video clips, recorded stories)

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 1.4 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks
activities.learning_activity: (b) Compose passages on specific contexts (structure: tenses –past progressive, present perfect, past perfect, present progressive, adjectives, adverbs, coordinators, prepositions, determiners, conjunctions)
suggested_methods: Jumbled sentences/paragraphs: Ask the students to rewrite jumbled sentences/paragraphs to form a meaningful passage about a specific context
Brainstorming: Guide the students in pairs through brainstorming different contexts for composing passages and presenting their work to the class
Discussion: Guide the students in groups through discussing important considerations when composing passages. Then, let each group present its work to the class
Composing: Guide the students through composing passages on the chosen contexts using appropriate grammar and vocabulary
activities.assessment_criteria: Passages on specific contexts are composed well

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of English language skills
activities.specific_competence: 1.4 Use appropriate grammar and vocabulary in oral and in written language tasks
activities.learning_activity: (c) Present messages in formal discussions on various issues using correct grammar and vocabulary
suggested_methods: Discussion: Provide the students with topics to discuss using correct grammar and vocabulary in initiating, intervening in and closing the discussions
Round table circle : Guide the students through participating in discussion on the selected topic using the round table circle and presenting their work to the class
activities.assessment_criteria: Messages in formal discussions on various issues using correct grammar and vocabulary are presented rationally

activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Read texts for comprehension
activities.learning_activity: (a) Express the main ideas from a text
suggested_methods: Discussion: Guide the students through identifying and discussing the main ideas of the texts read, and expressing the identified ideas orally and in writing
activities.assessment_criteria: The main ideas of texts are expressed properly

activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Read texts for comprehension
activities.learning_activity: (b) Infer meanings of unfamiliar words and idiomatic expressions from a given text
suggested_methods: Pair work: Guide the students in pairs through inferring the meanings of unfamiliar words and idiomatic expressions from a given text and presenting their work to the class
activities.assessment_criteria: The meanings of unfamiliar words and idiomatic expressions from a given text are inferred accurately

activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Read texts for comprehension
activities.learning_activity: (c) Relate the core messages from a text to real life experiences
suggested_methods: Group work: Provide the students with texts to read in groups, identify the core messages of the texts, relate them with their life experiences and present the work to the class
activities.assessment_criteria: The core messages from a text are related reasonably to real-life experiences

activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Comprehend oral messages with increasing difficulty
activities.learning_activity: (a) Synthesise ideas from a variety of oral sources
suggested_methods: Songs: Sing or play a song to the students while they listen to it attentively
Presentation: Present a topic or read aloud a passage/story to the students while they listen to it attentively
Pair work: Guide the students in pairs through synthesising ideas from the song and presentation or passage/story they have listened to. Then, let each pair present its work to the class
Discussion: Guide the students through discussing and synthesising ideas from various oral sources (e.g., radio, audio and video clips, television). Then, let each group present the work to the class
